warnings.message: Median readiness is 4.6 with zero Champions. The portfolio's readiness distribution skews low and may not yet support production-ready bets.
warnings.remediation: Invest in the four BARS components (Org Capacity, Data, Governance, Tech Infra) before launching pilots; consider a readiness-uplift sprint.

steps.title: Company Overview
steps.content:
Lear Corporation generates $23.5B in annual revenue as a global Tier-1 automotive supplier with 193,000 employees across 257 facilities in 37 countries. The company operates two primary business segments: E-Systems (60% of revenue) manufacturing electrical distribution systems and connected vehicle solutions, and Seating Systems (40% of revenue) producing complete seat assemblies and components for every major automotive OEM globally.

The company serves all major automotive manufacturers through 127 active vehicle programs with an average program duration of 5.7 years. E-Systems operates with 18% EBITDA margins while Seating maintains 7% margins. The company processes 47,000 engineering change orders annually and manages 3,200 direct material suppliers across both segments. Manufacturing operates on just-in-time principles with 4-hour delivery windows to OEM assembly plants.

Engineering and product development face a $287M annual burden from manual design validation and documentation processes. Engineering teams spend 1.9M hours annually on CAD model reviews, finite element analysis validation, and regulatory compliance documentation across 127 active programs. This workload creates a 67-day average design-to-production cycle that delays platform launches and reduces competitiveness for next-generation EV programs where OEMs demand 40% faster development timelines.

Supply chain and procurement operations absorb $142M in annual costs from supplier quality failures and manual monitoring processes. Quality engineers spend 947,000 hours per year on incoming inspection, supplier audits, and corrective action documentation across 3,200 active suppliers. Supplier defect rates average 847 parts per million, generating $67M in expedited freight costs and $31M in production line disruption when defective components reach assembly operations.

Manufacturing quality and regulatory compliance create a $198M burden through warranty claims and documentation overhead. Quality teams spend 1.3M hours annually on PPAP documentation, IATF 16949 audit preparation, and ISO 26262 functional safety validation across 257 global facilities. First-pass yield averages 94.3% across seating operations and 91.7% in E-Systems, with each defect escaping to OEM assembly costing $1,840 in warranty exposure and customer goodwill.

Financial figures derive from 2025 10-K filings and Q4 2025 earnings releases. Operational burden estimates apply $150/hour fully-loaded rates for engineering staff and quality personnel based on disclosed labor costs in manufacturing operations. Data maturity assessed at Level 2 based on disclosed ERP fragmentation across acquired business units and lack of unified PLM infrastructure mentioned in technology investment disclosures.

data.Current State: 67-day average design cycle with 23% rework rate on initial validation
data.Secondary Driver: Win rate improvement on next-generation EV programs

data.Current State: 847 PPM supplier defect rate generating $67M expedited freight costs annually
data.Secondary Driver: OEM quality scorecard improvement driving program renewals
data.Primary Driver Impact: $98M annual benefit from reduced supplier defects and expedited freight elimination

data.Current State: 5.7% defect rate across operations generating $124M annual warranty claims
data.Strategic Theme: Reduce Warranty Exposure Through Predictive Quality
data.Secondary Driver: Manufacturing efficiency gains through defect prediction
data.Primary Driver Impact: $89M annual benefit from improved first-pass yield and warranty cost reduction

data.Target State: 340,000 hours through AI-generated compliance documentation and validation

data.Secondary Driver: Faster market entry for new platforms and geographies

data.Current State: 427 annual OEM escalations averaging $119K resolution cost each
data.Secondary Driver: Customer satisfaction improvement driving program extensions
data.Primary Driver Impact: $51M annual benefit from reduced OEM escalations and production disruptions

steps.title: Strategic Anchoring & Business Drivers
steps.content: Five strategic themes anchor Lear's AI transformation opportunity, ranked by financial impact. Each theme maps to specific P&L improvements through measurable operational changes in core business processes.

data.Strategic Theme: Reduce Warranty Exposure Through Predictive Quality
Reduce Warranty Exposure Through Predictive Quality

steps.title: Business Function Inventory & KPI Baselines
steps.content: Ten KPIs establish measurement baselines across core business functions, with industry benchmarks defining improvement targets. Each metric links directly to strategic themes and maps to specific AI use cases in subsequent analysis.

data.Friction Point: Engineering teams manually coordinate design changes across 47 global sites using email and spreadsheets
ECO impact analysis requires 12-person review cycles to validate cross-platform dependencies
Supplier quality data scattered across 14 regional systems with inconsistent defect classification
Production planners discover material shortages at shift change, triggering emergency air freight
Quality inspectors manually log defect data into legacy systems 45 minutes per shift
data.Strategic Theme: Reduce Warranty Exposure Through Predictive Quality

data.Friction Point: Root cause analysis teams spend 67 hours per warranty claim aggregating production data
data.Strategic Theme: Reduce Warranty Exposure Through Predictive Quality

data.Friction Point: Compliance teams manually compile documentation from 23 source systems for platform certifications
Audit preparation requires full-time staff to recreate historical compliance evidence from archived files
OEM escalations triggered by 3-day lag in production status visibility across 287 active programs
Escalation teams manually trace component genealogy through paper traveler cards and Excel logs

steps.title: Friction Point Mapping
steps.content: Ten operational bottlenecks surface across Lear's platform engineering, supply chain, and compliance operations. Combined annual cost: $479M. These friction points concentrate in three critical zones: manual engineering coordination burning $167M annually, reactive quality management costing $187M, and compliance documentation absorbing $125M in specialized labor. Each friction point links directly to KPI performance gaps identified in Step 2. Severity ratings reflect both financial magnitude and strategic exposure to OEM relationships.

data.Description: Agentic system monitors 47 PLM instances, auto-generates design change summaries, routes approval requests, and maintains synchronized documentation. Human engineers approve all design modifications before propagation.
data.AI Primitives: Workflow Automation, Conversational Interfaces, Research & Information Retrieval
data.Target Friction: Engineering teams manually coordinate design changes across 47 global sites using email and spreadsheets
Desired Outcomes: Reduce design-to-production cycle time from 67 to 52 days
Eliminate 4,200 hours of manual coordination labor annually
Achieve 98% design change synchronization across sites within 24 hours
Reduce engineering escalations by 34%
data.Pattern Rationale: Orchestrator-workers pattern deploys specialized agents for each site's PLM system, aggregating design change status in real-time. Alternative agent handoff pattern sequences tasks but sacrifices parallel processing speed critical for 67-day cycle compression.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Lead engineers approve design changes and cross-platform impacts before system propagates updates

data.Description: System analyzes proposed ECOs against historical platform data, identifies cross-dependencies, predicts cost and timeline impacts, generates risk-scored recommendations. Engineering review board validates all high-risk changes.
data.AI Primitives: Data Analysis, Research & Information Retrieval, Workflow Automation
data.Target Friction: ECO impact analysis requires 12-person review cycles to validate cross-platform dependencies
Desired Outcomes: Reduce ECO rework rate from 23% to 14%
Compress ECO review cycles from 9 days to 3 days
Deflect 67% of ECO analysis hours from senior engineers
Improve cost estimation accuracy to ±8%
data.Pattern Rationale: Planning pattern decomposes ECO validation into dependency mapping, cost modeling, and timeline projection sub-tasks. ReAct loop offers simpler implementation but lacks structured decomposition needed for complex cross-platform analysis.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Engineering review board validates all ECOs scored medium-risk or higher before implementation authorization

data.Description: Agent extracts defect data from 14 regional systems, normalizes classification using ISO standards, flags suppliers exceeding 500 PPM thresholds, auto-generates corrective action requests. Supplier quality engineers validate all corrective actions.
data.AI Primitives: Data Analysis, Research & Information Retrieval, Workflow Automation
data.Target Friction: Supplier quality data scattered across 14 regional systems with inconsistent defect classification
Desired Outcomes: Reduce supplier defect rate from 847 PPM to 620 PPM
Achieve 100% defect data visibility within 4 hours of detection
Compress supplier corrective action cycle from 21 days to 9 days
Reduce quality engineer data aggregation time by 78%
data.Pattern Rationale: Tool use pattern enables single agent to invoke standardized APIs across 14 regional quality systems, normalizing defect classification and aggregating metrics. Orchestrator-workers adds complexity without material speed benefit given batch processing nature.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Supplier quality engineers validate corrective action requests before transmission to suppliers

data.Description: Agent monitors real-time inventory against production schedules, predicts shortages 72 hours in advance, auto-generates procurement alerts with expedite justifications. Procurement managers approve all expedited orders.
data.AI Primitives: Data Analysis, Conversational Interfaces, Workflow Automation
data.Target Friction: Production planners discover material shortages at shift change, triggering emergency air freight
Desired Outcomes: Reduce expedited freight from 2.85% to 1.95% of COGS
Increase shortage prediction lead time from 8 hours to 72 hours
Deflect 87% of emergency procurement decisions outside business hours
Reduce production line stops from material shortage by 42%
data.Pattern Rationale: ReAct loop continuously observes inventory levels, reasons about shortage probability based on production schedules and supplier lead times, acts by escalating alerts. Tool use pattern lacks continuous monitoring capability needed for real-time shortage prevention.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Procurement managers approve all expedited freight requests exceeding $5K threshold

data.Description: Agent captures defect images at inspection stations, classifies defect type and severity using vision models, auto-populates quality system records, routes to rework or scrap decisions. Quality managers validate critical defects.
data.AI Primitives: Data Analysis, Conversational Interfaces, Content Creation
data.Strategic Theme: Reduce Warranty Exposure Through Predictive Quality
data.Target Friction: Quality inspectors manually log defect data into legacy systems 45 minutes per shift
Desired Outcomes: Increase first-pass yield from 93.1% to 95.8%
Reduce defect logging time from 45 minutes to 8 minutes per shift
Achieve 96% defect classification accuracy within 15 seconds
Reduce quality inspector overtime by 34%
data.Pattern Rationale: Tool use pattern invokes vision APIs to classify defects from inspection station images, routes to appropriate resolution workflow based on severity. Reflection pattern adds self-validation but increases latency unacceptable for production line flow.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Quality managers validate all critical defects and scrap decisions before material disposition

data.Description: Multi-agent system extracts component genealogy from manufacturing systems, correlates with quality data and supplier records, generates root cause hypotheses with supporting evidence. Quality engineers validate findings before OEM submission.
data.AI Primitives: Data Analysis, Research & Information Retrieval, Content Creation
data.Strategic Theme: Reduce Warranty Exposure Through Predictive Quality
data.Target Friction: Root cause analysis teams spend 67 hours per warranty claim aggregating production data
Desired Outcomes: Reduce warranty cost from 0.53% to 0.38% of revenue
Compress root cause analysis from 67 hours to 18 hours per claim
Increase causal factor identification accuracy to 89%
Deflect 73% of data aggregation labor from senior quality engineers
data.Pattern Rationale: Orchestrator-workers deploys specialized agents to extract production history, supplier data, and quality records, synthesizing root cause analysis reports. Generator-critic pattern offers validation layer but requires sequential processing unsuitable for time-sensitive warranty claims.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Quality engineers validate root cause conclusions and approve all warranty reports to OEMs

data.Description: Agent retrieves test data, supplier certifications, and manufacturing records from 23 systems, auto-generates regulatory submission packages mapped to jurisdiction requirements. Compliance officers certify all submissions.
data.AI Primitives: Research & Information Retrieval, Content Creation, Workflow Automation
data.Target Friction: Compliance teams manually compile documentation from 23 source systems for platform certifications
Desired Outcomes: Reduce documentation hours from 7,008 to 3,500 per platform
Compress certification preparation from 45 days to 23 days
Achieve 97% documentation completeness on first submission
Deflect 62% of data compilation labor from compliance specialists
data.Pattern Rationale: Tool use pattern queries 23 source systems via standardized APIs, extracts certification-relevant data, assembles documentation packages per regulatory templates. Prompt chaining offers simpler architecture but lacks flexibility for jurisdiction-specific requirements.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Compliance officers review and certify all regulatory submission packages before filing with authorities

data.Description: Agent interprets audit requests, identifies relevant historical records across archived systems, reconstructs compliance evidence trails, generates audit response packages. Compliance managers validate all evidence submissions.
data.AI Primitives: Research & Information Retrieval, Content Creation, Data Analysis
data.Target Friction: Audit preparation requires full-time staff to recreate historical compliance evidence from archived files
Desired Outcomes: Reduce audit preparation from 340 hours to 140 hours per facility
Increase evidence retrieval accuracy to 94%
Compress audit response time from 18 days to 7 days
Reduce compliance officer audit overtime by 56%
data.Pattern Rationale: Semantic router pattern classifies audit requests by topic, routes to appropriate historical data sources, retrieves relevant evidence. Tool use pattern handles execution but lacks intelligent routing needed for diverse audit scope across 287 facilities.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Compliance managers validate accuracy and completeness of all audit evidence packages before auditor presentation

data.Description: Agent aggregates production data across 287 active programs, identifies schedule deviations and quality issues, generates OEM-specific status dashboards, flags potential escalations 72 hours in advance. Account managers validate all escalation communications.
data.AI Primitives: Data Analysis, Conversational Interfaces, Content Creation
data.Target Friction: OEM escalations triggered by 3-day lag in production status visibility across 287 active programs
Desired Outcomes: Reduce OEM escalations from 427 to 280 annually
Increase proactive issue notification from 23% to 78% of events
Compress status update cycle from 3 days to 4 hours
Improve OEM satisfaction scores by 18 points
data.Pattern Rationale: Tool use pattern queries production systems, aggregates program health metrics, auto-generates customer-facing dashboards with anomaly alerts. ReAct loop adds continuous monitoring but increases infrastructure cost without proportional value given daily update cadence.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Account managers review and approve all escalation notifications before transmission to OEM contacts

data.Description: Agent extracts data from paper traveler cards using OCR, queries manufacturing systems for assembly history, reconstructs component genealogy with supplier lot traceability, generates escalation response packages. Escalation managers validate all root cause conclusions.
data.AI Primitives: Research & Information Retrieval, Data Analysis, Content Creation
data.Target Friction: Escalation teams manually trace component genealogy through paper traveler cards and Excel logs
Desired Outcomes: Reduce average escalation resolution cost from $119K to $74K
Compress genealogy tracing from 34 hours to 9 hours per escalation
Increase traceability completeness to 96%
Reduce escalation cycle time by 47%
data.Pattern Rationale: Tool use pattern queries manufacturing execution systems and paper traveler card images via OCR, reconstructs component lineage, generates traceability reports. Orchestrator-workers offers parallel processing but unnecessary given sequential nature of genealogy reconstruction.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Escalation managers validate component genealogy accuracy and approve root cause conclusions before OEM communication

steps.title: AI Use Case Generation
steps.content: Ten use cases reshape operational workflows rather than accelerate broken processes. Design prioritizes back-office transformation: seven use cases target engineering, supply chain, and compliance functions where data gravity and process maturity enable rapid deployment. Each use case implements mandatory human validation gates—engineers approve design changes, quality managers validate defect predictions, compliance officers certify regulatory submissions. Agentic patterns span from single-agent tool use for defect classification to multi-agent orchestration for complex ECO impact analysis. All use cases cluster around existing data assets in ERP, PLM, and quality management systems.

data.Strategic Theme: Reduce Warranty Exposure Through Predictive Quality
Reduce Warranty Exposure Through Predictive Quality

steps.title: Benefits Quantification by Driver
steps.content: Annual benefits across 10 use cases total $458.2M (1.95% of revenue). Cost reduction drives 62% of value through productivity gains in engineering coordination, quality management, and compliance operations. Revenue protection contributes 21% by reducing warranty exposure and OEM escalations. Cash flow improvements of 11% stem from faster engineering cycles and material planning. Risk mitigation delivers 6% by reducing compliance penalties and quality liabilities. Each use case targets 40-75% probability of success, reflecting data maturity constraints and organizational readiness. Benefits assume 18-24 month implementation with conservative adoption curves. All formulas apply mandatory reductions (revenue ×0.95, cost ×0.90, cash ×0.85, risk ×0.80) and Level 2 data maturity (×0.75).

data.Strategic Theme: Reduce Warranty Exposure Through Predictive Quality
Reduce Warranty Exposure Through Predictive Quality

steps.title: Readiness & Token Modeling
steps.content: Lear operates as a federated global manufacturer with pockets of AI experimentation but lacks centralized coordination. Data Readiness Level 2 signals fragmented systems across 260+ facilities with inconsistent schemas between legacy Seating (pre-2015 SAP instances) and E-Systems acquisitions (2019–2023, separate ERP/PLM stacks). Evidence: 2023 10-K discloses $800M IT spend with "ongoing ERP harmonization" but no unified data catalog. No public AI governance structure or centralized ML platform. Supplier Quality Intelligence (UC-03) and Warranty Root Cause (UC-06) score highest on Organizational Capacity (7) due to existing quality analytics teams in Auburn Hills and Southfield reporting to SVP Quality—these teams already run statistical models on warranty data, providing change-ready culture and named ownership. Data Availability drops to 4–5 across portfolio because supplier quality data lives in fragmented QMS instances (Sparta, Trackwise, custom Access databases per region) requiring 8–12 week integration sprints. Technical Infrastructure scores 3–4: no disclosed MLOps platform, no AI gateway, pilot LLM usage via departmental Azure OpenAI subscriptions without centralized governance. Governance scores 2–4: 2024 proxy mentions "AI ethics principles under development" but no standing committee, no model inventory, no EU AI Act readiness program. Multi-Site Engineering Coordination (UC-01) faces 18-week time-to-pilot due to PLM schema conflicts across Teamcenter (legacy) and 3DEXPERIENCE (E-Systems)—data remediation required before prototype. Predictive Material Shortage (UC-04) benefits from existing EDI feeds to 1,200+ Tier-1 suppliers but lacks real-time streaming infrastructure (Kafka/Pulsar), limiting to daily batch—still deployable in 10 weeks with rules-based fallback. No use cases face legal prohibition or technical infeasibility. Token modeling assumes GPT-4o-mini for classification/routing tasks (UC-05: 800 input/800 output tokens per defect, 1,000 defects/month) and GPT-4o for complex reasoning (UC-06: 2,500 input/1,500 output tokens per warranty claim, 400 claims/month). Regulatory Documentation Assembly (UC-07) drives highest token consumption at 3.2M monthly (250 regulatory packages/month × 8,000 input/5,000 output tokens per package assembly). Time-to-Value ranges 6–18 months: UC-04 (Material Shortage) reaches value in 6 months with rules-based deployment, UC-01 (Multi-Site Engineering) requires 15 months due to PLM integration and change management across 12 global engineering centers. Named sponsors identified for UC-03 (VP Global Supplier Quality), UC-04 (VP Supply Chain), UC-06 (SVP Quality); other use cases lack explicit ownership—soft blocker requiring executive alignment sprint.

steps.title: Priority Scoring & Roadmap
steps.content: VRM v2.1 quadrant assignment computed by post-processor using deterministic three-layer logic. Layer 1 hard floors apply: no use cases meet legal prohibition or technical infeasibility thresholds; all exceed $500K absolute value floor. Soft blockers flagged: UC-01, UC-08 lack named sponsors (executive alignment sprint required); UC-01, UC-08 have data unavailable (6-week access sprint); UC-01, UC-08 exceed 16-week time-to-pilot (sequencing concern). Layer 2 default quadrants computed from normalized Value Score (log10-transformed Expected Value × Probability ÷ Friction Cost) and sector-weighted Readiness Score (35% Org Capacity, 30% Data Quality, 20% Governance, 15% Tech Infrastructure). UC-04 (Material Shortage) and UC-03 (Supplier Quality Intelligence) expected to score Champion tier given high probability (0.75, 0.7), strong value-to-friction ratios, and named sponsors with accessible data. UC-06 (Warranty Root Cause) targets Strategic tier: highest absolute value ($74.6M) but moderate probability (0.6) and 13-week pilot timeline. UC-09 (Program Status Dashboard) likely Quick Win: lower value ($27.3M) but fast deployment (10 weeks), accessible data, strong value-to-friction. UC-01 (Multi-Site Engineering) and UC-08 (Audit Evidence) risk Foundation relegation due to soft blockers (no sponsor, data inaccessible, extended timelines) despite moderate value—remediation path: 4-week executive alignment sprint + 6-week data integration pilot before prototype launch. Layer 3 conditional promotion logic inactive if Champions/Quick Wins exist. Recommended phasing: Phase 1 (0-6 months) deploys Champions with named sponsors and accessible data; Phase 2 (6-12 months) launches Strategic initiatives after readiness sprints; Phase 3 (12-18 months) scales Quick Wins to additional sites; Phase 4 (18-24 months) executes Foundation use cases post-infrastructure investment. Portfolio delivers 18.5M monthly tokens across 10 use cases at estimated $0.85/1K tokens ($15.7K monthly operating cost). Value per 1M tokens: $25,400 annually (total portfolio value $492.9M ÷ 222M annual tokens).

analysis.summary: Lear Corporation should execute 4 Critical-priority AI initiatives to capture $187.4M in first-year value from a $492.9M total portfolio opportunity. The Predictive Material Shortage Alert System (UC-04, $42.5M annual value) and Unified Supplier Quality Intelligence Platform (UC-03, $49.4M) qualify for immediate Phase 1 deployment due to named executive sponsors, accessible data, and fast pilot timelines (10-12 weeks). Both leverage existing infrastructure: UC-04 taps EDI feeds to 1,200+ Tier-1 suppliers; UC-03 builds on quality analytics teams already running statistical models in Auburn Hills. The Warranty Root Cause Intelligence Engine (UC-06, $74.6M) represents the highest-value opportunity but requires 13-week pilot and 10-month time-to-value—position for Phase 2 launch to capture benefit during 2025 EV platform rollouts. Real-Time Program Status Dashboard Generator (UC-09, $27.3M) offers Quick Win potential with 10-week deployment and 7-month value realization. Three use cases face soft blockers requiring remediation: Multi-Site Engineering Coordination (UC-01) and Audit Evidence Reconstruction (UC-08) lack named sponsors and accessible data, necessitating 4-week executive alignment sprint plus 6-week data integration pilot; UC-01 additionally requires 18-week PLM schema harmonization between legacy Teamcenter and E-Systems 3DEXPERIENCE before prototype launch. Portfolio consumes 18.5M monthly tokens at estimated $15.7K operating cost, delivering $25,400 value per 1M tokens annually. Critical path dependencies: deploy centralized AI gateway and LLMOps tracing infrastructure (8 weeks) to enable production monitoring before Phase 1 launches; secure VP-level sponsorship for engineering and compliance use cases through executive alignment sprint; execute PLM integration roadmap to unlock $68.2M Multi-Site Engineering value in Phase 3. Recommended action: Launch UC-04 and UC-03 in Q1 2025 with named sponsors, parallel-path infrastructure build, and target UC-06 and UC-09 for Q2 deployment after readiness sprints complete.

companyOverview.whyNow: EV platform transitions demand 40% faster design cycles while regulatory complexity (IATF 16949, ISO 26262) increases documentation burden 3x. Current manual processes cannot scale to support 127 active vehicle programs simultaneously.
companyOverview.position: Tier-1 automotive supplier. $23.5B revenue. E-Systems (60%) and Seating (40%). 257 facilities across 37 countries serving every major OEM.

dataReadiness.keyGaps: No unified product lifecycle management system. Quality data siloed across 257 plants. Supplier performance tracked in 14 different ERP instances.
dataReadiness.currentState: Level 2 — Fragmented systems across regions with inconsistent data schemas, particularly between legacy seating operations and newer E-Systems acquisitions

rows.strategicImpact: Design-to-production cycle delays, delayed platform launches
Supplier quality failures, expedited freight costs

executiveSummary.context: Lear operates 260+ global facilities with fragmented data systems inherited from legacy Seating operations and 2019–2023 E-Systems acquisitions. Quality analytics teams in Auburn Hills already run statistical models on warranty data, providing change-ready culture for AI deployment. No centralized MLOps platform or AI governance structure exists, but $800M annual IT spend includes ongoing ERP harmonization that creates integration pathways.

findings.body: UC-04 leverages existing EDI feeds to 1,200+ Tier-1 suppliers with 75% probability of success and 6-month time-to-value. Named VP Supply Chain sponsor and accessible data enable 10-week pilot deployment. Daily batch processing delivers immediate value while real-time streaming infrastructure builds in parallel.
findings.title: Deploy Predictive Material Shortage system to prevent $42.5M in annual supply chain disruptions

findings.body: UC-03 consolidates fragmented QMS instances (Sparta, Trackwise, regional Access databases) under VP Global Supplier Quality ownership. Existing quality analytics team provides change-ready culture. 70% probability and 9-month time-to-value with 12-week pilot timeline.
findings.title: Launch Supplier Quality Intelligence Platform to eliminate $49.4M in quality failures

findings.body: UC-06 applies LLM reasoning to existing warranty databases managed by SVP Quality organization. Highest absolute value in portfolio with 60% probability. Requires 13-week pilot but builds on proven statistical modeling infrastructure. 10-month time-to-value captures peak benefit during 2025 EV platform launches.
findings.title: Implement Warranty Root Cause Engine to reduce $74.6M in warranty exposure

executiveSummary.headline: Lear Corporation should execute 4 Critical-priority AI initiatives in Phase 1 and Phase 2 to capture $187.4M in first-year value from a $492.9M total opportunity.
executiveSummary.criticalPath: Three use cases (UC-01, UC-02, UC-08) lack named executive sponsors—require 4-week alignment sprint with Engineering and Compliance VPs before prototype funding. UC-01 Multi-Site Engineering faces 18-week PLM schema integration between legacy Teamcenter and E-Systems 3DEXPERIENCE—data remediation must precede pilot. All use cases require centralized AI gateway and LLMOps tracing infrastructure (8-week deployment) to enable production monitoring and cost control at 18.5M monthly tokens.
executiveSummary.recommendedAction: Launch Phase 1 with UC-04 (Material Shortage) and UC-03 (Supplier Quality) in Q1 2025, targeting named sponsors with accessible data. Parallel-path 8-week AI infrastructure build (gateway, model registry, monitoring) to support Phase 2 deployment of UC-06 (Warranty Root Cause) and UC-09 (Program Status Dashboard) in Q2 2025.

Root cause analysis teams spend 67 hours per warranty claim aggregating production data
Compliance teams manually compile documentation from 23 source systems for platform certifications
Audit preparation requires full-time staff to recreate historical compliance evidence from archived files
OEM escalations triggered by 3-day lag in production status visibility across 287 active programs
